description: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized crime group based in Baltimore, Maryland, active from the 1920s to the 1990s. Originally independent, it became a faction of the Gambino crime family in 1955. The group was involved in gambling, extortion, loan sharking, drug trafficking, and murder. It was led by the Corbi family for much of its existence.

country_links.evidence_quote: Antonio "Tony" Corbi went into hiding in 1923, and reportedly traveled to Mexico and Italy...
country_links.context: An individual member traveled to Italy to evade charges, but the organization itself does not have a footprint there.
